text: Over the last few years, there has been a significant paradigm shift in the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) using generative models in both supervised and unsupervised learning. The breakthroughs in generative models has led to the state of the art solutions to several real-world problems using image-to-image translation, image classification, multimedia synthesis and text analysis. This work presents a systematic review of the recent advancements in generative models along with the associated domain specific architectures, and the implementation for the various applications. In addition, this work highlights the recent breakthroughs of Large Language Models (LLMs) and diffusion-based image synthesis and their interdisciplinary applications. The main contribution of this work is the synthesis of the present works done in the field of generative models and connecting the recent works to showcase the future direction of this research area. Finally, this work highlights the need and importance of the Responsible AI and the ethics associated with the generative models and the future growth and acceptability of the generative models.
